project.name: pixelforge
project.description: 8-Bit Avatar Generator — Generate retro 8-bit pixel avatars using LLMs (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, DeepSeek, Gemini). Fully client-side React/TypeScript app.

nodes.name: LLM Provider Integration
nodes.summary: Manages connections to external LLM providers including provider selection, API URL and model configuration, and API key management. Uses a strategy pattern with five provider implementations (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, DeepSeek, Gemini) and persists configuration to localStorage and sessionStorage.

businessRules: API key must be set before generation (enforced by UI disabling FORGE button)
API key stored in sessionStorage only (cleared on tab close)
Provider URL and model auto-populate from presets on provider change
Custom URL/model overrides persist in localStorage and are preserved across provider switches
Settings auto-save to localStorage on every change
crossDomainInteractions: Provides configured provider type, URL, model, and API key to Avatar Generation domain
App.tsx wires useProviderConfig and useApiKey outputs into usePixelGeneration.generate() call

nodes.name: Avatar Generation
nodes.summary: The core domain that transforms a text prompt into a 16x16 pixel avatar via LLM. Handles prompt entry, character type selection via preset chips, LLM dispatch with provider routing, JSON response parsing with validation, and rendering the pixel grid as a CSS grid. Manages the full generation lifecycle with loading and error states.

businessRules: Cannot generate while already generating (generatingRef guard prevents race conditions)
Prompt must be non-empty and API key must be set for FORGE to be enabled
System prompt enforces 16x16 grid with valid 6-digit hex colors and NES-inspired palette
LLM response must conform to PixelMapResponse schema (palette, grid, label) or parse error is returned
401 API errors surface a specific 'Invalid API key' message to the user
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes provider configuration from LLM Provider Integration domain
Feeds generated PixelMapResponse into Avatar Export domain for downloads
Viewer state machine (empty → loading → result | error) is delegated to UI & Theming domain

nodes.name: Avatar Export
nodes.summary: Handles downloading generated pixel avatars in two formats: PNG (via OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale) and self-contained HTML file (with embedded CSS grid and inline styles). Both exports sanitize filenames for filesystem safety.

businessRules: PNG uses OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale of the 16x16 grid (256x256 output)
HTML export is a fully self-contained document with embedded CSS and inline styles
Filenames are sanitized using a lowercase alphanumeric + dash transform
Exports are best-effort (silently fail on errors, e.g. unsupported browser)
crossDomainInteractions: Consumes PixelMapResponse from Avatar Generation domain as source data
Export buttons are rendered inside ViewerShell from UI & Theming domain

nodes.name: User Interface & Theming
nodes.summary: Provides the app shell, layout structure, theme toggle (light/dark with localStorage persistence), and the viewer state machine (empty/loading/result/error). Orchestrates all UI components including header, config section, prompt section, and avatar viewer section.

businessRules: Theme is persisted to localStorage and applied via data-theme attribute on document element
Viewer follows a strict state machine: empty → loading → result | error
Re-Forge action resets the viewer back to empty state
App is divided into three labeled sections: 01 CONFIG, 02 PROMPT, 03 AVATAR
crossDomainInteractions: Wraps and displays components from all other domains
ViewerShell conditionally renders Avatar Grid (Avatar Generation), Loading Indicator, Error Display, and Export buttons (Avatar Export)

nodes.name: Configure Provider
nodes.summary: User selects an LLM provider from a dropdown, which auto-populates the API URL and model fields from presets. The user can override these values, and all settings are automatically persisted to localStorage.

nodes.name: Manage API Key
nodes.summary: User enters an API key in a password-masked input field with a show/hide toggle. The key is persisted to sessionStorage for the browser tab session only.

nodes.name: Generate Avatar
nodes.summary: The core generation flow: user enters a prompt (or selects a preset chip), optionally chooses a character type, clicks FORGE or presses Cmd+Enter. The system dispatches to the configured LLM provider, parses the JSON response, validates it, and renders the pixel grid. Shows loading state during generation and error state on failure.

domainMeta.entryPoint: FORGE button click or Cmd+Enter keyboard shortcut

nodes.name: Export as PNG
nodes.summary: User clicks the Download PNG button, which renders the pixel map onto an OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale, converts to a PNG Blob, and triggers a browser download with a sanitized filename.

nodes.name: Export as HTML
nodes.summary: User clicks the Download HTML button, which builds a fully self-contained HTML document with the pixel grid rendered as CSS divs, inline styles, and the character label. The HTML is served as a Blob download with a sanitized filename.

nodes.name: Toggle Theme
nodes.summary: User clicks the theme toggle button in the header, which switches between light and dark mode. The theme preference is persisted to localStorage and applied as a data-theme attribute on the document root element.

nodes.name: Navigate Viewer States
nodes.summary: The viewer component acts as a state machine with four states: empty (initial placeholder), loading (animated pixel indicator), result (avatar grid + download buttons + re-forge), and error (error message with optional dismiss). User can trigger a re-forge to return to empty state.

domainMeta.entryPoint: Viewer state change triggered by generation lifecycle

nodes.name: Select Provider
nodes.summary: User opens a custom dropdown and selects one of five LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenAI-Compatible, DeepSeek, Gemini). Selection auto-populates URL and model fields from preset defaults unless overridden.

nodes.name: Update API URL
nodes.summary: User can override the auto-populated API URL with a custom endpoint. Overrides are tracked and preserved when switching providers.

nodes.name: Update Model
nodes.summary: User can override the auto-populated model name with a custom model identifier. Overrides are tracked and preserved when switching providers.

nodes.name: Auto-Save Settings
nodes.summary: Provider type, API URL, and model are automatically saved to localStorage on every change via a useEffect. A temporary 'Settings saved locally' notice is shown for 3 seconds.

nodes.name: Enter API Key
nodes.summary: User types their API key into a password-masked input field. The field supports show/hide visibility toggle.

nodes.name: Toggle Key Visibility
nodes.summary: User clicks SHOW/HIDE button to toggle the API key input between masked (password) and visible (text) states.

nodes.name: Persist to Session
nodes.summary: API key is persisted to sessionStorage so it persists across page refreshes within the same browser tab session but is cleared on tab close.

nodes.name: Enter Prompt
nodes.summary: User types a character description into the textarea prompt field. The prompt is used to construct the full generation request sent to the LLM.

nodes.name: Select Avatar Type
nodes.summary: User clicks a preset chip (Wizard, Knight, Robot, Astronaut, Ninja, Elf) which auto-fills the prompt with a detailed description and sets the character type label. Clicking the same chip again clears the selection.

nodes.name: Trigger FORGE
nodes.summary: User clicks the FORGE button or presses Cmd+Enter (Ctrl+Enter) to initiate avatar generation. Button is disabled when generating, prompt is empty, or no API key is set.

nodes.name: Dispatch Generation
nodes.summary: App.tsx constructs the full prompt by prepending type context, then calls generate() on usePixelGeneration hook with the prompt and provider config. The hook guards against concurrent generations via a ref flag.

nodes.name: Route to Provider
nodes.summary: llmClient.generate() looks up the appropriate provider implementation from a lazy-initialized registry using the provider type string and delegates the PixelGenRequest to it.

nodes.name: Call Provider API
nodes.summary: The selected provider implementation (AnthropicProvider, OpenAIProvider, OpenAICompatibleProvider, DeepSeekProvider, or GeminiProvider) sends an HTTP request to its specific API endpoint with provider-specific headers, system prompt, and user prompt.

nodes.name: Parse LLM Response
nodes.summary: parsePixelMapResponse() validates the raw LLM JSON output against the PixelMapResponse schema: checks palette is array of valid hex colors, grid is square array of hex colors, label is non-empty string. Returns Result tagged union with parse errors on failure.

nodes.name: Set Pixel Map State
nodes.summary: On successful parsing, the PixelMapResponse is stored in React state via setPixelMap, which triggers a re-render to display the avatar grid.

nodes.name: Render Avatar Grid
nodes.summary: AvatarGrid renders the 16x16 pixel map as a CSS grid with dynamic gridTemplateColumns. Each cell is a colored div. The grid is displayed at 256x256px. Shows character label and metadata (grid size + color count).

nodes.name: Show Loading Indicator
nodes.summary: During generation, the viewer displays an animated pixel loading indicator with 'GENERATING...' text, providing visual feedback to the user.

nodes.name: Handle and Display Errors
nodes.summary: On generation failure (API error, network error, parse error), the error message is surfaced in ErrorDisplay with an optional dismiss button. 401 errors get a user-friendly 'Invalid API key' message.

nodes.name: Click Download PNG
nodes.summary: User clicks the DOWNLOAD PNG button, which calls exportAsPng() with the current pixel map, type, and name.

nodes.name: Render to OffscreenCanvas
nodes.summary: Creates an OffscreenCanvas at 16x scale of the pixel grid (256x256 for a 16x16 grid) and draws each pixel cell as a filled rectangle using the grid's hex colors.

nodes.name: Download PNG Blob
nodes.summary: Converts the OffscreenCanvas to a PNG Blob, creates an object URL, generates a sanitized filename (pixelforce-{type}-{name}.png), and triggers a programmatic download via a hidden anchor element.

nodes.name: Click Download HTML
nodes.summary: User clicks the DOWNLOAD HTML button, which calls htmlExport() with the grid data, label, prefix, and username.

nodes.name: Build HTML Document
nodes.summary: buildAvatarHTML() generates a fully self-contained HTML document with embedded CSS styles (grid layout, centering, monospace font, beige background), inline pixel divs with validated hex colors, and an escaped character label.

nodes.name: Download HTML Blob
nodes.summary: Wraps the generated HTML string in a Blob with text/html MIME type, creates an object URL, generates a sanitized filename (pixelforce-{prefix}-{username}.html), and triggers a programmatic download via a hidden anchor element.

nodes.name: Click Theme Toggle
nodes.summary: User clicks the DARK/LIGHT button in the header to toggle between dark and light themes. The button label updates to reflect the opposite theme.

nodes.name: Apply Theme
nodes.summary: The active theme is applied to the UI by setting the data-theme attribute on document.documentElement via a useEffect running on theme state changes.

nodes.name: Persist Theme Preference
nodes.summary: Theme selection is persisted to localStorage under the key 'pixelforge-theme' so the user's preference is restored on subsequent visits.

nodes.name: Show Empty State
nodes.summary: When no generation has been performed, the viewer shows a decorative icon and the text 'ENTER A PROMPT & FORGE YOUR PIXEL' as an initial call to action.

nodes.name: Show Loading State
nodes.summary: During active generation, the viewer switches to the LoadingIndicator component showing an animated pixel with 'GENERATING...' text.

nodes.name: Show Result State
nodes.summary: On successful generation, the viewer displays the AvatarGrid (pixel map with metadata), action buttons for PNG download, HTML download, and a RE-FORGE button to start fresh.

nodes.name: Show Error State
nodes.summary: On generation failure, the viewer displays the ErrorDisplay component with the error message and an optional dismiss button.

nodes.name: Re-Forge Action
nodes.summary: User clicks the RE-FORGE button to reset the viewer back to the empty state, clearing the pixel map, error, and generation state via the reset() callback.

edges.description: Provides configured provider type, API URL, model, and API key used by the generation flow
Generated PixelMapResponse is consumed by PNG and HTML export flows
App shell renders provider config and API key UI sections
Viewer shell dispatches generation, shows loading/error states, and renders avatar grid
Viewer shell renders download buttons for PNG and HTML export
